retrieval_route: Official Finnish justice-site result surface followed by direct PDF text scan
ai_or_process_text_snippet: Tämän aukeaman tiivistelmien teossa on osittain hyödynnetty tekoälyä.
bounded_finding: The Eastern Finland Administrative Court 2025 report contains one bounded internal-use AI mention tied to summary production, but the text pass found no institution-specific confirmation of AI-drafted administrative appeals, fabricated case references, or workload response burden matching the Yle row.
next_route_implication: General court annual-report scanning should no longer be treated as a likely threshold-changing Finland route unless a named institutional section or appendix is identified first.
paper_use: Strengthens a methods/limitations note showing that official judiciary AI mentions can be operational or editorial rather than event-companion evidence for procedural contamination.
dashboard_hook: Official-companion saturation panel; false-positive route-cost board
caution: Do not overread one internal-use AI sentence as confirmation of the broadcaster-described phenomenon.

retrieval_route: Official Finnish justice-site result surface followed by direct PDF text scan
ai_or_process_text_snippet: Toivoa myös sopii, ettei tekoälyn ja muiden uusien innovaatioiden hyödyntämismahdollisuuksia selviteltäessä unohdeta perustyökalujen ylläpidon ja kehittämisen tärkeyttä.
bounded_finding: A 2024 hallinto-oikeus annual report discusses AI and innovation at a generic court-modernization level, but the text pass found no bounded support for AI-generated appeal contamination or for institution-specific response measures matching the Yle case.
next_route_implication: Generic court-modernization discussions belong to governance-context sidecars, not to exact Finland phenomenon confirmation.
paper_use: Feeds reviewer-safe prose on why justice-system AI modernization talk must remain separate from evidence that a named court or police legal unit received AI-drafted appeals.
caution: Do not flatten generic innovation commentary into proof of the procedural phenomenon.

bounded_finding: The Supreme Court speech is useful as official judicial AI-governance context on translation, anonymization, controllable sources, transparency, and responsibility questions, but it does not confirm the Yle-described appeals phenomenon or a Finland-specific administrative-court response.
next_route_implication: If reused, this route should support contextual framing about courts and AI rather than be counted as case corroboration for the Finland row.
paper_use: Useful for introduction/methods text about why courts discuss AI seriously even when event-specific corroboration is still missing.
caution: Do not cite judicial AI-governance speech as event-level corroboration.

ai_or_process_text_snippet: Tekoälyn ja automaation kehitysohjelma vahvistaa hallinnonalan kykyä hyödyntää uusia teknologioita prosessien sujuvoittamiseen ja palveluiden parantamiseen.
bounded_finding: The justice-administration development-program page confirms a formal AI-and-automation governance program, but the page contains no `valitus` or `hallinto-oikeus` event-companion support for contaminated administrative appeals.
next_route_implication: Official justice-administration AI-program pages should be treated as environment context only unless they name a complaint/appeal phenomenon or institution-specific mitigation response.
paper_use: Supports a clean distinction between macro-level AI program adoption and micro-level case-companion evidence needed for Table 1E Panel A promotion.
caution: Do not upgrade the Finland row from programmatic AI-adoption language alone.
